generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoLiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR detects objects in the vehicle's proximity via point cloud generation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera provides 3D environmental context for object tracking and path projection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle State Sensors capture real-time metrics such as ego vehicle speed and steering angle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuationController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed commands into motor voltage commands via Vedder Electronic Speed Controller"
]

instance SteeringServoController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Manages steering angle adjustments for the vehicle's lower-level chassis"
]

instance AccControlLogic : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Computes lead vehicle distance and determines desired speed based on ISO 15622 Performance Class I ACC"
]

// C3: Data Transmitted
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDARSensor
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point clouds processed via DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering for object tracking"
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthCamera
    base:desc "3D environmental context data for object tracking and path projection"
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Continuous streams of speed and steering angle data synchronized with 100 ms accuracy"
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDARSensor, IntelDepthCamera
    base:desc "Detected objects in vehicle's proximity characterized by position, velocity, and size"
]

// C4: Data
instance RawLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    base:desc "Unprocessed LiDAR point cloud data for initial object detection"
]

instance ProcessedLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR data after DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering for object tracking"
]

instance EgoVehicleState : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    base:desc "Real-time speed and steering angle metrics of the ego vehicle"
]

instance ObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData
    base:desc "Historical object tracks and ego vehicle state data stored for analysis"
]

instance ProjectedPathData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraData
    base:desc "3D CAD-derived vehicle and environment representations for real-time rendering"
]

// C5: Models
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "DBSCAN clustering model for noise filtering of LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DBSCANProcessingEnabler
]

instance KalmanFilterModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filtering model for state estimation of detected objects"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEnabler
]

instance Iso15622AccControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ISO 15622 Performance Class I ACC logic for distance-based speed adjustment"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AccControlEnabler
]

// C6: Enablers
instance JetsonOrinHardware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer for onboard processing and AI acceleration"
]

instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for software component integration and communication"
]

instance PythonPrototyping : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python 3.8 for rapid prototyping of perception and control algorithms"
]

instance CPlusPlusNodeLifecycle : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++ for ROS2 node lifecycle management and system integration"
]

instance MATLABSimulinkSimulation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink for complex system simulations including point cloud clustering"
]

instance DBSCANProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom DBSCAN implementation for LiDAR point cloud clustering"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringModel
]

instance KalmanFilterEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filtering implementation for object state estimation"
    DTDFVocab:enables KalmanFilterModel
]

instance AccControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 15622 compliant ACC control logic implementation"
    DTDFVocab:enables Iso15622AccControlModel
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom Data Translation Functional Entity ensuring interoperability between ROS2 and Wi-Fi protocols"
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackData, ProjectedPathData
]

instance WiFiCommunication : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "UDP-based Wi-Fi communication protocol for inter-entity communication between vehicle and Digital Twin"
]

instance ROS2Communication : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 middleware for intra-entity communication between vehicle components"
]

// C7: Services
instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of ego vehicle speed, steering angle, detected object tracks, and projected driving path via 3D visualization libraries"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTime3DVisualizationInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, or trigger emergency braking with 100ms latency"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterUpdateAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC control logic from vehicle ECUs to cloud infrastructure, reducing onboard resource constraints"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLogicOffloadAction
]

instance ReportGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatically generates human-readable reports of object tracks and vehicle state as time-series data"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalReportInsight
]

// C8: Insights/Actions
instance RealTime3DVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D display of ego vehicle state, detected object tracks, and projected driving paths"
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote adjustment of ACC settings (e.g., desired speed) based on real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic emergency braking triggered by detected safety threats"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HistoricalReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automated reports of object tracks and vehicle state data for developers and operators"
]

// C9: Environment
instance PhysicalVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoLiDARSensor, IntelDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor,
              MotorActuationController, SteeringServoController
]

instance DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains JetsonOrinHardware, ROS2Middleware, PythonPrototyping,
              CPlusPlusNodeLifecycle, MATLABSimulinkSimulation,
              DataTranslationFE, WiFiCommunication, ROS2Communication
]

instance UserInterfaceEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RealTimeVisualizationService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT sends control commands to the physical twin through:
  - **ACC Controlling FE (Digital Twin Entity)**: Computes desired speed based on lead vehicle distance and sends commands via Wi-Fi (UDP) to the vehicle’s Jetson Orin Nano.
  - **Emergency Braking Commands**: Triggered via the Control Panel UI, executed by the Actuation FE to halt the motor.
  - **Remote Management Commands**: Enables/disables ACC or modifies desired speed, transmitted over Wi-Fi with <100 ms latency (3GPP 22.185 compliance)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates with the following time-scale specifications:
  - **Real-time Data Collection**: Data streams (e.g., perception cycles, sensor readings) are processed at real-time rates, with a maximum end-to-end latency of 100 ms between the vehicle and the Digital Twin Entity (per 3GPP 22.185).
  - **Clock Synchronization**: Devices within the DT system must maintain a 100 ms accuracy in time synchronization for event ordering and data integrity, though NTP-based synchronization is not explicitly detailed (only accuracy requirement is specified).
  - **Data Processing Frequency**: Point cloud clustering and tracking occur at unspecified but high-frequency rates (likely >10 Hz), while ACC control updates are synchronized with the perception cycle (real-time, but no exact Hz value is provided)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is structured as a **multi-layered entity-based architecture** adapted from ISO 23247:
  - **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensor preprocessing (LiDAR/Depth Camera), actuation (motor/steering), and data translation.
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data collection, ACC control logic, and report generation.
  - **User Entity**: Provides 3D visualization and control panel interfaces (split into two sub-FEs: Presentation FE and User Interface FE).
  - **Cross-System Entity**: Implements Data Translation FE for inter-entity communication (e.g., ROS2/Wi-Fi protocols). The architecture deviates from ISO 23247 by introducing a **Physical Twin Entity** (not defined in the standard) to encapsulate automotive-specific resources."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates across the following lifecycle stages:
  - **Design Phase**: Used for cyber-physical experimentation and validation of ACC system requirements (e.g., ISO 15622 compliance).
  - **Operational Phase**: Enables real-time monitoring, remote management, and optimization of ACC during vehicle operation.
  - **No explicit Predictive Maintenance Stage**: While predictive maintenance is mentioned as a general DT application in the background, it is not a specific service or lifecycle phase for this ACC case study."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity considerations include:
  - **Verification**: Ensures real-time data streams meet latency (<=100 ms) and clock synchronization (<=100 ms) requirements.
  - **Validation**: Validates ACC logic against ISO 15622 standards and system performance against perception cycle constraints.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: No explicit safety margins are mentioned, though the system adheres to strict timing constraints for safety-critical operations.
  - **Data Accuracy**: Point cloud processing (DBSCAN/Kalman) and sensor fusion ensure high-fidelity object tracking and ego vehicle state estimation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs the following technical connections:
  - **Communication Protocols**:
    - **Intra-Entity**: ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for intra-entity communication within the vehicle’s onboard system.
    - **Inter-Entity**: Wi-Fi (UDP) for communication between the vehicle and Digital Twin Entity, ensuring low-latency (<100 ms) data transfer.
  - **Clock Synchronization**: Devices must synchronize clocks with 100 ms accuracy (hardware constraint), though NTP-based synchronization is not explicitly detailed.
  - **Data Serialization**: Data Translation FE ensures compatibility across ROS2 and Wi-Fi protocols, including unit conversions and message alignment."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed as follows:
  - **Physical Twin**: Embedded on the 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle, using NVIDIA Jetson NX/Orin Nano for compute.
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosted on cloud infrastructure (not specified) for offloading ACC control logic.
  - **User Interface**: Deployed on a remote device (e.g., laptop/desktop) via web-based ROS2 clients or desktop applications."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT integrates horizontally with:
  - **External Systems**: No explicit mention of integration with other DTs or external IoT systems (e.g., V2V communication). Potential future extensions could include:
    - **Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) Protocols**: Adherence to 3GPP 22.185 for low-latency communication with other autonomous vehicles.
    - **Cloud Services**: Offloading data storage/reporting to external databases."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership is ambiguous but inferred as:
  - **Physical Twin Data**: Owned by the vehicle manufacturer/developer (sensors/actuators).
  - **DT Data**: Collected and processed by the DT service provider (cloud-based storage/reporting). Privacy considerations are not addressed in the document, but real-time data transmission over Wi-Fi may require encryption (not specified)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT follows **ISO 23247** for architectural design (entity-based structure, Data Translation FE), **ISO 15622** for ACC control logic, **ROS2** for middleware, and **3GPP 22.185** for V2V communication constraints. Deviations include the introduction of a **Physical Twin Entity** (not defined in ISO 23247) and a split User Interface FE."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Safety and security are addressed as follows:
  - **Safety**:
    - **Latency Constraints**: End-to-end latency ≤100 ms ensures deterministic control for ACC (critical for autonomous driving).
    - **Clock Synchronization**: ±100 ms accuracy prevents timing-based attacks or data corruption.
    - **ISO 15622 Compliance**: ACC logic adheres to safety standards for autonomous vehicles.
  - **Security**:
    - **Encryption**: Not explicitly mentioned; potential Wi-Fi communication may require TLS/SSL for data integrity.
    - **Remote Management**: Emergency braking commands are triggered via UI with explicit user consent (human-in-the-loop).
    - **Data Integrity**: Data Translation FE ensures standardized message formats to prevent malformed commands."
]
